title: Edge Security, WAF, Hotlinking, Headers, and Failure Containment · Language Lens: Waf Rate Bot Boundary
summary: A public distribution trusts Referer as authorization, caches private errors, lacks browser security headers, and forwards bot bursts to an expensive origin. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the waf rate bot boundary increment in layered edge-security and abuse-control plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
Provide captions, transcripts, audio description where needed, player keyboard operation, status, quality fallback, and representative network tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Waf Rate Bot Boundary · Theory Waf Rate Bot Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6896 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to layer application authorization, WAF, origin protection, headers, and safe error caching; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
